surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `high priority`. Maintainer `high priority` label confirms the bug is important and acknowledged.

linked_prs.title: Update mixture_same_family.py
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: PR was approved by COLLABORATOR fritzo and CI passed, but went stale after the author did not add a regression test as requested. A GitHub stale-bot labeled it 'Stale' in May 2022 and it was subsequently closed. The fix itself is correct — fritzo's APPROVED review confirms this.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: COLLABORATOR fritzo submitted an APPROVED review on 2022-03-07 with: 'Thanks for the fix! It would be great to have a simple regression test in test/distributions/test_distributions.py if you feel up to writing one.' CI was all green. PR went stale and was closed by stale-bot in June 2022 without being merged.

linked_prs.title: Bugfix to MixtureSameFamily's _pad_mixture_dimension
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: A second attempt by the same author (CJMenart) in 2024. CONTRIBUTOR soulitzer (not COLLABORATOR/MEMBER/OWNER) approved the PR and triggered @pytorchbot merge multiple times. pytorchmergebot said 'Merge started' but GitHub API confirms merged=false and merge_commit_sha does not exist in the repository. Issue remains OPEN, confirming the fix did not land. The @pytorchbot merge trigger by a CONTRIBUTOR (not a high-trust committer) may have been blocked by merge policies.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: CONTRIBUTOR soulitzer approved the PR on 2024-02-02 and triggered @pytorchbot merge on 2024-02-02 and 2024-02-06. pytorchmergebot confirmed 'Merge started' on 2024-02-06. However, GitHub API reports merged=false and the merge_commit_sha (98dd2e45923f11122f7ca9d0900a5ea2231c651e) does not exist on main. Issue #73792 remains OPEN, confirming the fix did not land.

maintainer_signals.author: mruberry
maintainer_signals.quote: Would you extend this issue with a small code snippet that demonstrates the bug and shows the before/after with the proposed fix?

maintainer_signals.author: fritzo
maintainer_signals.quote: Thanks for the fix! It would be great to have a simple regression test in test/distributions/test_distributions.py if you feel up to writing one.

confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label present — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs → +0.25. Running total: 0.75. Step 2: repro script present — complete runnable code block provided by the reporter that demonstrates the bug (throws 'RuntimeError: only tensors with up to 64 dims are supported' due to numel() returning a product of dimensions instead of a count of dimensions) → +0.15. Running total: 0.90. Step 3: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 1.00. Step 4: fix_scope is single_function — the fix is a one-line change replacing `numel()` with `len()` in `_pad_mixture_dimensions` within a single function in torch/distributions/mixture_same_family.py → +0.10. Running total: 1.10. Step 5: issue open 2+ years (2022 to 2026) with no resolution → +0.05. Running total: 1.15. Step 6: COLLABORATOR fritzo approved PR #73864 (approved_fix signal) → +0.20. Running total: 1.35. Step 7: no blocking signals. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0]. Final: 1.00.
ground_truth_delta: This finding closely matches the positive_71774 profile: a silent correctness bug with a specific named function (_pad_mixture_dimensions), a clear repro script, a COLLABORATOR-approved fix PR that was never merged, triaged label, and a single-function scope. Key differences from 71774: (1) the fix is even simpler — a one-line change rather than a kernel-level fix, making revival more tractable; (2) there were two separate fix attempts (PR #73864 and #118947), both failing to merge despite approvals, suggesting the primary obstacle is process friction (CLA, stale-bot, merge-label requirements) not technical disagreement; (3) no scoped_bug signal from maintainers — the bug affects any user with non-trivial batch shapes. More actionable than 71774 in terms of fix complexity; on par for signal quality.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #73864 ('Update mixture_same_family.py') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size. The PR received a COLLABORATOR APPROVED review — the fix approach is already validated.

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #73792. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: Revive the approach from PR #73864, which received an APPROVED review from COLLABORATOR fritzo. The only blocker was a missing regression test. Port the fix to current main (the API may have shifted since 2022) and add the test fritzo requested in test/distributions/test_distributions.py.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/distributions/test_distributions.py -k MixtureSameFamily`. COLLABORATOR fritzo specifically asked for 'a simple regression test in test/distributions' — this is a gating requirement.
contribution_path.warning: Do not submit without the regression test — fritzo\'s APPROVED review was conditional on adding it.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #73792' and 'Revives #73864 and #118947' in the body. Tag @mruberry @fritzo for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
